meaning: vi.坐立不安,烦躁,慌张,(不安地或心不在焉地)弄,玩弄 vt.使烦乱,使不安n.坐立不安,烦躁,不安定
examples: You could take this as a license to fidget.
We fidget more when we lie—actually, the opposite is true.
Moving around throughout the day can inhibit a tendency to fidget and chatter.
Whenever he noticed me looking at him he would fidget or check his watch.
While the majority of teens admitted they are most likely to fidget with a radio while driving, they see it as less dangerous than other factors causing accidents.
